a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:2:"id";s:6:"thnihe";s:4:"name";s:4:"code";s:6:"parent";i:0;s:8:"children";a:0:{}s:8:"settings";a:6:{s:11:"executeCode";b:1;s:9:"signature";s:32:"e9ab15b709debc6986f859c1a437d1b1";s:7:"user_id";i:1;s:4:"time";i:1772251117;s:4:"code";s:17765:"
What We Monitor / Agitators & Mixers

Asset type

Agitator & mixer monitoring

SAM4 monitors chemical reactors, blending vessels, and mixing tanks from the motor control cabinet — detecting bearing degradation, seal failure indicators, imbalance, and gearbox issues without opening the vessel or entering the process area.

Request a Demo See What We Detect

At a glance

Monitoring method
Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA)
Sensor location
Motor control cabinet (MCC)
Voltage range
Low and medium voltage
Drive type
DOL, soft starter, VFD
Equipment types
Reactor agitators, blenders, top-entry mixers, side-entry mixers
Faults detected
4 fault types
Typical lead time
3–10 weeks before failure

Why monitor agitators and mixers

Inside a sealed vessel — invisible to inspection, critical to production

Agitators operate inside sealed, pressurised, or hazardous vessels. Physical inspection requires vessel isolation, draining, and confined space entry. Most agitators run without any condition monitoring — failures are discovered only when product quality drops or the seal leaks.

€30–150K

typical cost of an unplanned agitator failure — including vessel isolation, cleaning, repair, and production loss.

3–7 days

average downtime for an agitator failure in a chemical plant, including vessel decontamination and recommissioning.

0%

of agitators in a typical chemical plant have continuous condition monitoring before SAM4.

What SAM4 detects on agitators and mixers

Four fault types from one sensor location

Fault type What's happening How SAM4 detects it Typical lead time Confidence
Bearing degradation Motor bearing and gearbox bearing wear, lubrication breakdown Characteristic bearing fault frequencies in the motor current spectrum 4–12 weeks Full
Seal wear Mechanical seal degradation leading to leakage risk, shaft wear Progressive load profile change as friction characteristics shift 3–8 weeks Full
Imbalance Product buildup on impeller blades, erosion, or damaged impeller 1× running speed component increase in the current spectrum 2–8 weeks Full
Gearbox faults Gear tooth wear, bearing degradation within the gearbox assembly Gear mesh frequencies and bearing fault frequencies in current spectrum 3–10 weeks Full

Detection capability depends on drive topology, sampling rate, load variation, and operating conditions. See ESA detection constraints.

Agitator and mixer detections

Real faults caught on agitators and mixers

Bearing Chemicals

Bearing fault on reactor agitator

Progressive bearing degradation detected on a batch reactor agitator. Replaced during scheduled vessel turnaround — avoided emergency isolation.

Read detection story →
Seal wear Chemicals

Seal deterioration on mixing vessel

Load profile change flagged developing mechanical seal issue. Seal replaced before leakage into process area.

Read detection story →
Imbalance Chemicals

Product buildup on blending agitator

Imbalance from product accumulation on impeller blades detected. Vessel cleaned during next batch changeover.

Read detection story →
Gearbox Chemicals

Gearbox wear on top-entry agitator

Gear mesh frequency changes flagged developing gear tooth wear. Gearbox scheduled for overhaul before tooth breakage.

Read detection story →
Bearing Chemicals

Bearing degradation on side-entry mixer

Storage tank mixer bearing fault identified remotely. Replaced without draining the vessel.

Read detection story →
Process Chemicals

Viscosity change detected via agitator load

Load signature shift revealed unexpected viscosity change in batch reactor. Process team alerted before product quality deviation.

Read detection story →
See all detection stories →

Installation on agitators and mixers

30 minutes. No access to the vessel required.

1

Open the motor control cabinet

SAM4 installs at the MCC — the same panel your electricians already access. No confined space entry, no scaffolding, no process shutdown.

2

Clip sensor onto motor supply cables

Current and voltage sensors clip directly onto the existing cabling. No wiring changes. No interruption to the asset's operation.

3

Connect and commission

The SAM4 gateway connects via cellular (4G/LTE) — no dependency on your IT network. Monitoring starts immediately. First diagnostic results within 48 hours.

Installation photo: SAM4 sensor clipped onto agitator motor supply cables in an MCC
Full installation guide → Security & network architecture →

Where agitators and mixers are monitored

Industries using SAM4 on agitators and mixers

Chemicals

Reactor agitators, blenders, mixing vessels. Monitoring inside sealed and hazardous process environments.

Related asset types

Other asset types SAM4 monitors

Pumps

Process pumps, transfer pumps, circulation pumps

Compressors

Process air and refrigeration compressors

Fans & Blowers

Cooling tower fans, ventilation systems

Transmissions

Gearboxes, couplings, belt drives, chain drives
See all asset types →

See SAM4 monitoring agitators and mixers

A 30-minute demo shows SAM4 running on agitators and mixers like yours — with real fault data and real diagnostics from chemical process operations.

Request a Demo Start Your Asset Audit

a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:2:"id";s:6:"t6nb7u";s:4:"name";s:4:"code";s:6:"parent";i:0;s:8:"children";a:0:{}s:8:"settings";a:6:{s:11:"executeCode";b:1;s:9:"signature";s:32:"e22637190cc313871c0ecad6bc0df840";s:7:"user_id";i:1;s:4:"time";i:1772251117;s:4:"code";s:18769:"
What We Monitor / Archimedes Screws

Asset type

Archimedes screw monitoring

SAM4 monitors Archimedes screw pumps from the motor control cabinet — detecting bearing degradation, trough wear indicators, drive chain faults, and efficiency loss. Critical for preventing pollution events at wastewater inlet works.

Request a Demo See What We Detect

At a glance

Monitoring method
Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA)
Sensor location
Motor control cabinet (MCC)
Voltage
Low voltage
Drive type
DOL, soft starter
Equipment types
Open and enclosed Archimedes screws
Faults detected
5 fault types
Typical lead time
2–10 weeks before failure

Why monitor Archimedes screws

"A screw failure at an inlet works means a pollution event"

Archimedes screws at wastewater inlet works are the first line of defence against overflow and pollution. When they fail, raw sewage bypasses screening and enters the river or coastal waters. These assets are hard to inspect — submerged, slow-turning, and often inaccessible. Continuous monitoring is the only reliable approach.

€200K+

potential pollution fine per event — plus reputational damage and regulatory action.

24/7

continuous monitoring, no manual inspection needed — critical for safety-critical assets.

2–10 weeks

typical detection lead time — sufficient to plan replacement before failure.

What SAM4 detects on Archimedes screws

Five fault types from one sensor location

Fault type What's happening How SAM4 detects it Typical lead time Confidence
Bearing degradation Upper and lower bearing wear, raceway damage, lubrication breakdown Characteristic bearing fault frequencies appear in the motor current spectrum 4–10 weeks Full
Trough wear Screw-to-trough clearance increase from wear, affecting load path Progressive load pattern changes as contact area shifts — deviation from baseline load signature Weeks–months Partial
Drive chain faults Chain stretch, sprocket wear, misalignment, link failure Characteristic chain frequencies in the current spectrum as teeth engagement changes 2–8 weeks Full
Efficiency loss Wear-driven flow reduction, bearing friction increase, misalignment losses Power consumption tracking relative to lift height and flow rate — continuous deviation analysis Continuous Full
Overload Debris, sediment, or rags causing sustained load increase and motor strain Motor current rise above baseline for the given head and flow conditions Hours–days Full

Detection capability depends on drive topology, sampling rate, load variation, and operating conditions. See ESA detection constraints.

Screw detections

Real faults caught on Archimedes screws

Bearing degradation Water & Wastewater

Bearing fault detected in inlet screw

Bearing fault frequencies identified weeks before mechanical failure. Bearing replacement scheduled during planned maintenance, preventing pollution event.

Read detection story →
Chain fault Water & Wastewater

Drive chain wear detected on Archimedes screw

Chain frequency sidebands appeared in the current signature indicating progressive wear. Chain replaced preventing sudden failure.

Read detection story →
Overload Water & Wastewater

Sediment buildup detected in inlet screw

Motor current elevation detected above baseline for observed flow conditions. Inspection revealed sediment blockage requiring immediate maintenance.

Read detection story →
See all detection stories &rarrow;

Installation on Archimedes screws

30 minutes. No screw access required.

1

Open the motor control cabinet

SAM4 installs at the MCC — the same panel your electricians already access. No confined space entry, no process disruption.

2

Clip sensor onto motor supply cables

Current and voltage sensors clip directly onto the existing cabling. No wiring changes. No interruption to the screw's operation.

3

Connect and commission

The SAM4 gateway connects via cellular (4G/LTE) — no dependency on your IT network. Monitoring starts immediately. First diagnostic results within 48 hours.

Installation photo: SAM4 sensor clipped onto motor supply cables inside an MCC panel
Full installation guide → Security & network architecture →

Energy monitoring

Not just fault detection. Energy optimisation too.

Worn screws and incorrect operating speeds waste significant energy at wastewater treatment works. SAM4 continuously tracks power consumption relative to lift height and flow, identifying efficiency losses from wear, misalignment, or operational changes.

5–15% energy waste from worn screws and bearing friction
Continuous monitoring enables optimal speed control and energy management

Power vs. flow tracking

SAM4 continuously measures power draw relative to observed flow and lift height — enabling detection of wear-driven efficiency losses.

Efficiency deviation alerts

Automatic alerts when power consumption rises above baseline for the given operating conditions — indicating bearing friction or wear.

Speed optimisation

Data to support optimal operating speed decisions — backed by months of continuous power and flow data.

Where Archimedes screws are monitored

Industries using SAM4 on Archimedes screws

Water & Wastewater

Inlet works, low-head pumping stations, screening systems. Critical safety-critical equipment preventing pollution events.

Related asset types

Other asset types SAM4 monitors

Pumps

Centrifugal, positive displacement, and submersible pumps

Fans & Blowers

Ventilation fans, cooling fans, process blowers

Conveyors

Belt, screw, and chain conveyors in materials handling

Low Voltage Motors

AC induction motors up to 1kV
See all asset types →

See SAM4 monitoring Archimedes screws

A 30-minute demo shows SAM4 running on inlet screws like yours — with real fault data and real early warning before failure.

Request a Demo Start Your Asset Audit
